generatedAtPolicy: Deterministic. Regenerate with npm run postman:parity:write.

target.claim: Full Postman script import parity requires zero unsupported rows in default import mode.

scriptRunOrder.title: Use scripts to add logic and tests to Postman requests

dynamicVariables.title: Use dynamic variables to return randomly generated data

vault.title: Reference vault secrets in Postman scripts

sendRequest.title: Use scripts to send requests in Postman

testExpect.title: Writing tests and assertions in scripts

workflows.title: Customize request order in a collection run

mock.title: Reference requests and examples in local mock servers

state.title: Persist state across requests in local mock servers

grpcScripts.title: Test and debug values in gRPC requests using JavaScript in Postman

graphqlHttp.title: Make a GraphQL call with an HTTP request

newman-sandbox-v1.notes: Existing importer and collection-runner corpus for the brokered HTTP sandbox subset.

newman-cancellation.notes: Cancellation fixture for pending async sandbox work.

differential-http-core.notes: HTTP-only fixture intentionally limited to APIs supported by both PostMeter and Newman.

protocol-script-hooks.notes: GraphQL Before query/After response and gRPC Before invoke/On message/After response fixture for imported protocol scripts.

local-mock-scripts.notes: Local mock script fixture covering pm.mock matching, saved examples, path variables, async pm.state, and imported mock script events.

websocket-script-audit.notes: Current official-docs audit recording that WebSocket and Socket.IO request docs do not expose saved/exported script hooks.

desktop-observation-template.notes: Template for manually recorded Postman Desktop observations for non-Newman surfaces.

real-world-import-corpus.notes: Broad Postman import corpus covering auth-heavy scripts, reviewed package references, dynamic variables, cookies, visualizers, vault metadata, GraphQL, gRPC, local mocks, runRequest workflows, and file/binary body references.

adversarial-sandbox-v1.notes: Security regression tests for constructor/prototype escape, dynamic code confinement, package-loader abuse, broker mutation, raw host access, visualizer isolation, mock-state abuse, protocol-stream floods, oversized output, and infinite async work.

statuses.implemented: Implemented for the documented row and covered by at least one fixture.
statuses.partial: Partially implemented; fixture coverage may only prove the currently supported subset.
statuses.not-started: Required by the contract but not implemented yet.
statuses.intentional-strict-gap: Intentionally stricter than Postman for security or product-scope reasons; full parity cannot be claimed while active in default mode.
statuses.needs-desktop-observation: Needs current Postman Desktop behavior capture before implementation can be considered compatible.
statuses.needs-source-audit: Needs an official-source audit before implementation scope is final.

rows.target: HttpOnly script readability and mutation parity
GraphQL HTTP body, variables, and scripts
Native desktop gRPC transport execution for imported gRPC requests
Adversarial sandbox corpus is release-gated for production claim regressions.
Desktop-only Postman surfaces have a recording path for manual observations.
Differential runner targets newman@6.2.2 for Newman-compatible surfaces.
Full 1:1 Postman script compatibility claim fails until default import mode has zero unsupported rows.
Real-world-style public/user import corpus covers complex imported Postman scripting surfaces.
Postman Package Library and external package fetch/review workflow can satisfy missing imported package references before first run.
HTTP collection, folder, and request scripts run in Postman hierarchy.
Load tests stay outside sandbox v1 scripting unless a separate contract is written.
Advanced Postman auth helpers, proxy, and client-certificate parity for brokered script requests
User-granted imported file and binary attachment bindings for brokered script requests
Generated official-docs parity inventory exists and is validation-gated.
Vault prompt, denial, reset, and audit UX
Audit current Postman WebSocket script support
rows.notes: Official WebSocket and Socket.IO request docs do not document saved/importable script hooks; PostMeter records this and does not invent an incompatible hook surface.
